growth_records.medium: MSS-CDM (modified Stainer-Scholte chemically defined medium; acetic acid pH control)

up.method: principal C+N source; total fermentation time defined by glutamate exhaustion (drop in O2 consumption)

sec.method: B. pertussis secretes a significant proportion of N as arginine; experimentally validated

sec.compound: purine nucleosides (adenine, adenosine, cytidine, deoxycytidine, deoxyguanosine, thymidine)
sec.method: secreted as alternative N sinks; experimentally validated

growth_records.cite: Branco dos Santos et al. Probing the Genome-Scale Metabolic Landscape of Bordetella pertussis, the Causative Agent of Whooping Cough. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1128/AEM.01528-17. [PMC5648915]
growth_records.snippet: Rich media Tohama I MSS-CDM ... Initial OD650 0.168, Final OD650 8.7, Fermentation duration 45h40, Avg doubling time 7.0 h, PT yield 15.2 +/- 1.3 mg/liter, FHA yield 124.9 mg/liter (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Table 1, pH-controlled aerobic batch fermentation, strain Tohama I, MSS-CDM. Avg generation time (doubling) = log2(final OD650/initial OD650)/total fermentation time = 7.0 h (reported). mu = ln2/7.0 = 0.6931/7.0 = 0.0990 h-1 (calculated). Glutamate 120 mM is main C+N source (monohydrate 21.79 g/L + glutamic acid 1.579 g/L); full medium recipe in paper Table 3 (not fully transcribed here). Arginine + purine nucleoside secretion validated experimentally (arginine by Megazyme K-Large kit; nucleosides by LC-MS). PT/FHA are protein virulence-factor titers, not metabolic exchange rates. Model-derived (not per-condition experimental) energetics: Y_ATP 3.6 mg DCW/mmol ATP; non-growth maintenance 9.21 mmol ATP/gDW/h.

up.method: main C+N source; fermentation ended at glutamate exhaustion

growth_records.cite: Branco dos Santos et al. Probing the Genome-Scale Metabolic Landscape of Bordetella pertussis, the Causative Agent of Whooping Cough. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1128/AEM.01528-17. [PMC5648915]
growth_records.snippet: Rich media Tohama I MSS-CDM ... 0.159, 7.8, 39h00, 6.7, PT 22.5 +/- 3.9, FHA 112.9 (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Table 1 second MSS-CDM Tohama I fermentation (H3PO4 for pH control). Doubling 6.7 h reported; mu = ln2/6.7 = 0.6931/6.7 = 0.1034 h-1. Distinct fermentation run from the acetic-acid MSS-CDM record.

growth_records.cite: Branco dos Santos et al. Probing the Genome-Scale Metabolic Landscape of Bordetella pertussis, the Causative Agent of Whooping Cough. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1128/AEM.01528-17. [PMC5648915]
growth_records.snippet: Rich media Tohama I IMP-CDM ... 0.155, 8.3, 47h00, 7.9, PT 29.0 +/- 2.6, FHA ND (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Table 1. Doubling 7.9 h reported; mu = ln2/7.9 = 0.6931/7.9 = 0.0877 h-1. IMP-CDM detailed composition in Table 3 (not transcribed).

growth_records.cite: Branco dos Santos et al. Probing the Genome-Scale Metabolic Landscape of Bordetella pertussis, the Causative Agent of Whooping Cough. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1128/AEM.01528-17. [PMC5648915]
growth_records.snippet: Rich media Tohama I IMP2-CDM ... 0.177, 8.3, 41h15, 7.2, PT 36.7 +/- 2.2, FHA 103.2 (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Table 1. Doubling 7.2 h reported; mu = ln2/7.2 = 0.6931/7.2 = 0.0963 h-1. IMP2-CDM is a rationally redesigned medium giving highest PT titer (36.7 mg/L, >2-fold over baseline).

growth_records.cite: Branco dos Santos et al. Probing the Genome-Scale Metabolic Landscape of Bordetella pertussis, the Causative Agent of Whooping Cough. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1128/AEM.01528-17. [PMC5648915]
growth_records.snippet: Rich media Tohama I IMP2-CDM-AA as in MSS-CDM ... 0.167, 8.7, 43h15, 7.6, PT 32.3 +/- 1.7 (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Table 1. Doubling 7.6 h reported; mu = ln2/7.6 = 0.6931/7.6 = 0.0912 h-1.

growth_records.cite: Branco dos Santos et al. Probing the Genome-Scale Metabolic Landscape of Bordetella pertussis, the Causative Agent of Whooping Cough. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1128/AEM.01528-17. [PMC5648915]
growth_records.snippet: Rich media Tohama I LCMSSB ... 0.104, 4.1, 33h00, 6.2, PT 2.7 +/- 1.8, FHA 14.1 (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Table 1. Doubling 6.2 h reported (fastest of the set); mu = ln2/6.2 = 0.6931/6.2 = 0.1118 h-1. LCMSSB is the industrial reference medium giving low antigen (PT) titer.

growth_records.cite: Branco dos Santos et al. Probing the Genome-Scale Metabolic Landscape of Bordetella pertussis, the Causative Agent of Whooping Cough. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1128/AEM.01528-17. [PMC5648915]
growth_records.snippet: Minimal media Tohama I SS ... 0.138, 1.8, 63h00, 17.2, PT 0.8 +/- 0.4, FHA 10.5 (Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Table 1, classical Stainer-Scholte minimal medium (glutamate-based, no added amino acids). Doubling 17.2 h reported; mu = ln2/17.2 = 0.6931/17.2 = 0.0403 h-1. Low biomass (final OD650 1.8) and low PT.

growth_records.medium: CIT-NH3 (citrate as sole C source, ammonia as N source)

up.method: growth on citrate demonstrates a functional TCA cycle in B. pertussis Tohama I

growth_records.cite: Branco dos Santos et al. Probing the Genome-Scale Metabolic Landscape of Bordetella pertussis, the Causative Agent of Whooping Cough. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1128/AEM.01528-17. [PMC5648915]
growth_records.snippet: Minimal media Tohama I CIT-NH3 ... 0.007, 6.5, 159h00, 16.1, PT <0.5, FHA 27.4 (Table 1). Citrate as sole C source and ammonia as N source; biomass yield over 3-fold higher than SS.
growth_records.notes: Table 1. Doubling 16.1 h reported; mu = ln2/16.1 = 0.6931/16.1 = 0.0430 h-1. Growth on citrate (sole C) + ammonia (sole N) confirms functional TCA cycle. Biomass yield >3-fold higher than SS medium; very low antigen (PT<0.5).

growth_records.cite: Branco dos Santos et al. Probing the Genome-Scale Metabolic Landscape of Bordetella pertussis, the Causative Agent of Whooping Cough. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1128/AEM.01528-17. [PMC5648915]
growth_records.notes: Table 1, alternative strain 18323 in MSS-CDM (acetic acid pH control). Doubling 6.7 h reported; mu = ln2/6.7 = 0.6931/6.7 = 0.1034 h-1.

growth_records.cite: Branco dos Santos et al. Probing the Genome-Scale Metabolic Landscape of Bordetella pertussis, the Causative Agent of Whooping Cough. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2017). DOI:10.1128/AEM.01528-17. [PMC5648915]
growth_records.notes: Table 1, strain 18323 in IMP2-CDM. Doubling 8.5 h reported; mu = ln2/8.5 = 0.6931/8.5 = 0.0815 h-1. Highest final OD650 (10.1) of the set.
